reasoning: The PCI DSS control 2.2.3 requires different security levels for different functions within a system component. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that roles and permissions are appropriately restricted (RBAC), network access is controlled (NetworkPolicies), pods are not running with unnecessary privileges (Pod Security), and services are not exposed unnecessarily (Service Type). These checks ensure that different components or functions within a Kubernetes cluster have appropriate security boundaries.
title: (continued) If a system component contains primary functions that need different security levels,...
